tags.name: The Wall
tags.notes: Located in Mirbat area, Dhofar region, ~70km from Salalah. Wall dive along rocky coastal cliff face. Operated by local Salalah dive operators. Seasonal: Oct-May only due to khareef monsoon.

tags.name: Aquarium
tags.notes: Located in Mirbat area, Dhofar region. Site type corrected from 'wall' to 'reef': multiple sources describe this as a coral garden/reef with large table corals, not a vertical wall. Named for dense fish life resembling an aquarium. Shore-accessible, shallow coral garden 8-18m. Seasonal: Oct-May.

tags.name: China Wreck (direction 150deg)
tags.notes: Located in Mirbat area, Dhofar region. Vessel identity: possibly SS Kiva (also spelled Kheva), a pilgrim passenger vessel that departed Bombay for Mecca in 1893 with ~979 pilgrims. A fire broke out in the cargo hold; the vessel steered toward Mirbat port and sank approximately 300 metres offshore. Oman Observer (2021) and Muscat Daily (2021) both reported on the Oman Ministry of Heritage and Tourism's documentation survey of 'a 128-year-old wreck off Mirbat' identified as the Kiva; dive operators in the region have subsequently linked this to the China Wreck site. CONFLICT: one operator source describes the wreck only as 'a former steam-powered ship scattered over ~80m N-S' without naming the Kiva; the 'China Wreck' name may alternatively refer to porcelain/china cargo found at the site. Vessel identity is probable but not fully resolved — do not state as confirmed fact. The '150-year-old' claim in previous description was unsourced and has been removed. Direction '150deg' used as navigational bearing from shore. Seasonal: Oct-May.

tags.name: ABT Salalah
tags.notes: Located near Salalah city (Dhofar region), coordinates different from Mirbat cluster (~60km west of Mirbat sites). Site name 'ABT Salalah' appears to be named after the local operator ABT Divers, not a vessel name. Vessel identity UNKNOWN — no source confirms the actual ship name, tonnage, or sinking date for this specific site. Depth: local operator listings and regional sources indicate 30+ metres — not the 25m in OSM. Difficulty: Advanced (depth exceeds typical intermediate range, offshore conditions). Do not conflate with the 'China Wreck' at Mirbat (different site, different coordinates). Wreck conflict: site identity unresolved. Further research needed.

tags.name: Flamingo Beach: Side Entry
tags.notes: Located in Mirbat area, Dhofar region. Flamingo Beach is a sheltered cove with coral reef. Multiple entry points mapped as separate sites. 'Flamingo Beach' not found by that name in operator site lists — may be a local/informal name for a cove in the Mirbat area. Seasonal: Oct-May.

tags.name: Flamingo Beach: New Entry
tags.notes: Located in Mirbat area, Dhofar region. Flamingo Beach sheltered bay reef site. Multiple entry points mapped separately. 'Flamingo Beach' not found by that exact name in operator site lists — may be local name. Seasonal: Oct-May.

tags.name: Flamingo Beach: Beach Entry
tags.notes: Located in Mirbat area, Dhofar region. Shore/beach entry reef dive. 'Flamingo Beach' not found by that exact name in operator site lists — may be local name for a cove near Mirbat. Seasonal: Oct-May.

tags.name: Eagle Bay
tags.notes: Located in Mirbat area, Dhofar region, ~70km from Salalah. Site type corrected from 'wreck' to 'reef': multiple independent sources (GetYourGuide, SSI dive guide, beautifulsalalah.com) confirm Eagle Bay is a reef/coral site, not a wreck. Features sandy patch, coral bank (south), rocky outcrops (north). Depth 5-15m. Beginner-friendly, minimal currents. Seasonal: Oct-May.

tags.name: Dibba Rock Reef
tags.notes: Located at Dibba, northern Musandam Peninsula, Oman side of the UAE/Oman border. Coordinates confirmed in Oman (not UAE). Rocky reef with walls, caves, and drop-offs to 40m per SSI guide; 18m depth in OSM is conservative. Suitable for beginners though currents can be present during tidal changes. Accessible from both UAE and Oman side operators. Protected marine area.

tags.name: Snoopy Island Reef
tags.notes: GEOGRAPHIC ISSUE: Coordinates place this site at ~25.49N, 56.36E — this is Al Aqah, Fujairah, UAE, NOT Oman. The site name 'Snoopy Island Reef' combined with 24m depth matches 'Snoopy Deep Reef' (also called 'New Reef'), an artificial wreck/reef site ~500m east of Snoopy Island. Snoopy Island itself (shallow, 5-8m, beginner) is different from this 24m Advanced site. Site type corrected from 'reef' to 'wreck' as Snoopy Deep Reef is an artificial reef built around a purposely-sunk dive boat. Difficulty 'Advanced' is correct for the 24m deep site. OSM placed this under Oman in error; actual jurisdiction is UAE/Fujairah.
